context: hev ask is an Astro docs search integration with one committed knowledge graph shared by the browser overlay, HTTP read API, Go ask CLI, MCP server, and embeddable Go library. Authors build the graph offline with ask kg or the bundled Claude Code skill, commit it, and verify anchors so every result and answer links to rendered sections. Runtime search degrades from agentic SSE to keyword JSON when no server key is available.

glossary.definition: An Astro integration that adds a ⌘K search overlay with instant keyword search and an optional Claude-powered agentic search path.
An offline-built, git-committed JSON artifact of distilled section nodes (with verbatim facts, summaries, and source links) that the agentic loop reasons from.
A bounded Claude tool-use loop, triggered by Enter, that opens sections it needs and then streams a grounded answer with inline deep links.
The dependency-free, keyless path that scores chunks by token overlap (widened by the glossary) and returns ranked section deep links as the reader types.
A document slice split on a heading, carrying the heading, cleaned prose, and a basePath+slug+#anchor URL; the unit hev ask indexes.
The #id appended to a section URL, generated with github-slugger so it matches Astro's rendered heading id.
The default export of @hev/ask: the Astro integration configured with an options object in astro.config.mjs.
The Astro component that renders the ⌘K command palette and posts queries to the search endpoint.
The on-demand route the integration injects; returns keyword JSON or an agentic SSE stream depending on mode and key presence.
The server-side secret that enables the agentic path and the offline graph build; never exposed to the browser.
A sha256 of concatenated chunk text used to decide whether the graph needs regeneration; build skips the model call when a committed graph matches.
The same slug library Astro's renderer uses, adopted as hev ask's single dependency to keep generated anchors byte-identical to rendered heading ids.
A node mode flag: source-primary nodes (reference/API sections) are read verbatim by the agent, while agent-primary nodes are read as paraphrased summaries.
Terms with aliases and definitions in the knowledge graph that widen keyword queries (e.g. k8s → kubernetes) before retrieval.
The cap on how many search tool rounds the agentic loop runs before it must stop and answer (default 4).
A Node/Cloudflare/Vercel adapter required because the search endpoint renders on demand; static-only builds cannot serve search.
The CLI subcommand that builds the site and checks anchors, coverage, and literal fidelity against the committed graph without calling the model.
Keyless routes under the search endpoint that expose glossary terms, section nodes, and overview context from the committed graph.
The published machine-readable contract for the search, answer, and knowledge-graph read endpoints.
The reusable Go package behind the ask CLI, exposing graph read, endpoint client, and search helpers for other tools.
The Go ask command that deterministically emits section input for knowledge-graph distillation.
The Go ask command that combines a distillation file with deterministic facts, overview, nodes, and content hash to write kg.json.
The Go ask command that builds or reads dist output and checks rendered anchors, graph coverage, and literal fidelity.
The Go ask command that hash-gates the corpus and calls the KG model only when the committed graph is stale.
The v3 command surface shipped by @hev/ask; it reads the committed graph, runs MCP, and owns ask kg producer commands.
The ask CLI command group that builds, assembles, and verifies the committed knowledge graph.
The stdio server that exposes hev ask glossary, section, overview, search, and answer operations as tools for coding agents.
The JavaScript bin wrapper in @hev/ask that resolves an explicit binary, platform binary package, or source checkout for the ask CLI.
The dependency-free Go command surface in pkg/ask that lets another CLI mount hev ask docs commands.

suggestions: How can my coding agent query these docs locally?
How do I run hev ask as an MCP server?
How do I rebuild and verify the knowledge graph?
How do I embed hev ask docs commands in another Go CLI?

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.summary: @hev/ask ships the ask binary as the main CLI. Consumer commands read the committed graph for agents, while producer commands under ask kg build, assemble, and verify that graph; hev-ask-kg remains only as a deprecated forwarding alias.

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Building the graph
nodes.summary: ask kg build, corpus, assemble, and verify run from the Astro site root. Build is hash-gated, corpus/assemble expose the deterministic distillation seam, and verify checks anchors, coverage, and literal fidelity.

facts.literal:
ask kg build        # one-shot build; needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY only when stale
ask kg corpus       # emit sections for a keyless skill/model distillation
ask kg assemble     # assemble .hev-ask/kg.json from that distillation
ask kg verify       # build the site and verify anchors, coverage, fidelity

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Claude Code skill
nodes.summary: The bundled build-kg skill runs ask kg corpus, writes the model distillation, and runs ask kg assemble. Because the model step happens in Claude Code, it does not spend the user's ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and still produces the same kg.json shape.

facts.literal:
ask kg corpus       -> .hev-ask/kg-input.json
...writes .hev-ask/kg-distill.json...
ask kg assemble     -> .hev-ask/kg.json

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Distribution
nodes.summary: @hev/ask exposes ask as the main bin and hev-ask-kg as a deprecated alias. The launcher checks HEV_ASK_BINARY, platform optional binary packages, and finally a source checkout fallback used for local development.

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Flags
nodes.summary: The CLI flags cover local graph paths, remote endpoints, JSON output, local search limits, producer corpus settings, the KG build model, corpus/assemble scratch paths, and verify behavior such as --skip-build and --strict.

facts.literal:
ask --kg-path .hev-ask/kg.json --json search "openapi"
ask --endpoint https://askhev.com/api/ask mcp
ask kg build --collection docs --collection guides --chunk-heading-depth 2
ask kg verify --skip-build

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Go library
nodes.summary: pkg/ask exposes both low-level graph helpers and a dependency-free CommandGroup for embedding docs commands in another Go CLI. The same package also provides endpoint clients, graph building, verification, and ServeMCP.

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: MCP
nodes.summary: ask mcp starts a stdio MCP server exposing the same glossary, section, overview, search, and answer surface as the CLI. Use --kg-path for local offline tools or --endpoint when an agent needs the deployed graph or answer tool.

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Package scripts
nodes.summary: Site package scripts should use ask kg build and ask kg verify. Existing hev-ask-kg scripts continue working through the deprecated alias, but new integrations should call ask kg directly.

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Reading the graph
nodes.summary: The ask read commands load .hev-ask/kg.json by default and can switch to a deployed /api/ask endpoint with --endpoint. Local search is keyless, while ask answer requires the endpoint because it streams through the remote agentic SSE path.

facts.literal:
ask glossary list
ask glossary get "knowledge graph"
ask sections list --group API
ask section get api/endpoint#knowledge-graph-reads-get
ask overview
ask search "read endpoints"
ask --endpoint https://askhev.com/api/ask answer "what read routes exist?"
ask mcp

nodes.title: CLI
nodes.heading: Where it runs
nodes.summary: Producer commands run locally or in CI with filesystem access, while the deployed site reads the committed graph through virtual:hev-ask/kg. The Astro build hook invokes ask kg build when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is available and falls back to the committed graph on failure.

nodes.title: Configuration
nodes.heading: Options
nodes.summary: Options Option Type Default Description collections string[] — Content collection name(s) to index and search. Without it the endpoint errors and the integration warns at build. basePath string '/docs/' Slug → URL prefix…

nodes.summary: Tuning notes Chunk granularity: raise chunkHeadingDepth to 3 (the default) for finer section anchors on long pages; drop to 2 if your ### sections are too small to stand alone as results. Latency: lower maxIterations to…

nodes.heading: TypeScript

nodes.title: Configuration
nodes.heading: What the integration does
nodes.summary: What the integration does When Astro starts up (astro:config:setup) the integration: injects the endpoint route, pointing at @hev/ask/endpoint, with prerender: false so it renders on demand; registers two virtual module…

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.summary: Documents the route tree: the base route serves suggestions, keyword JSON, and agentic SSE, while read subroutes expose glossary entries, section nodes, and overview context from the committed graph without an API key.

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Agentic response (SSE)
nodes.summary: Agentic response (SSE) When a key is present and mode is agentic, the endpoint responds with content-type: text/event-stream and streams the answer as it is generated. Each event is a named SSE frame: event: search data:…

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Errors
nodes.summary: Lists JSON and SSE error shapes, including invalid request bodies, missing read resources, index build failures, and failures that happen after an agentic stream has already started.

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Index lifecycle
nodes.summary: The endpoint builds and caches its chunk index on first request, then compares the live content hash against the committed graph hash. A mismatch logs a one-time warning telling maintainers to run ask kg build.

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Keyword response (JSON)
nodes.summary: Keyword response (JSON) Keyword mode returns a 200 JSON envelope: { "results": [ { "title": "Concepts", "heading": "The agentic search loop", "url": "/docs/concepts#the-agentic-search-loop", "group": "Overview", "snippet…

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Knowledge graph reads (GET)
nodes.summary: Documents the keyless graph-read routes for listing glossary terms, resolving a glossary term or section, listing sections by group, and retrieving overview context. It defines the lightweight section summary shape and the URL-encoding rule for section IDs.

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Mode selection
nodes.summary: Mode selection The endpoint decides what to run: Empty query → { results: [], query: "", model } (JSON). mode: "keyword", or no API key → keyword JSON, mode: "keyword". mode: "agentic" but no key → keyword JSON plus a wa…

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Request
nodes.summary: Request POST with a JSON body: { "query": "how does autoscaling work", "mode": "agentic" } Field Type Description query string The search query. Empty or whitespace returns an empty result set. mode 'keyword' \ 'agentic'…

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: Suggested questions (GET)
nodes.summary: Suggested questions (GET) GET /api/ask returns the knowledge graph's baked-in suggestions and the loop model — no query, no model call: { "suggestions": ["How does the knowledge graph stay fresh?"], "model": "claude-hai…

nodes.title: Search endpoint
nodes.heading: The API key
nodes.summary: The API key The endpoint resolves ANTHROPICAPIKEY from, in order: the adapter runtime env (locals.runtime.env, e.g. Cloudflare), process.env, then import.meta.env. Set it wherever your host injects server secrets; it is…

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.summary: The committed .hev-ask/kg.json artifact: distilled section nodes with verbatim facts and source links, the glossary and overview, how each field is used, and how it degrades when absent. The knowledge graph is a single…

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.heading: Degradation
nodes.summary: Degradation The graph is read defensively. If the file is missing or malformed, or it is an older node-less (v1) artifact, the integration uses it as-is: with no nodes the agentic loop falls back to keyword-style search,…

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.heading: Fields
nodes.summary: Fields Field Type Description version 2 Schema version. generatedAt string ISO timestamp stamped by the build (Node time, not edge). contentHash string sha256 of the concatenated chunk text. The freshness gate. context s…

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.heading: How each field is used
nodes.summary: How each field is used overview + nodes are injected into the agentic loop's system prompt (prompt-cached). The agent opens the sections it needs and answers from their summaries, quoting facts verbatim for exact strings…

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.heading: KnowledgeNode
nodes.summary: KnowledgeNode Field Type Description id string Equals the chunk id, so it maps 1:1 to a real anchor. summary string The model's paraphrase — what the agent reasons from. facts Fact[] Verbatim literals (flags, code, ident…

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.heading: Regenerating
nodes.summary: After docs changes, regenerate and commit kg.json with the Claude Code skill or ask kg build. The Astro build hook also invokes ask kg build when a key is present, and ask kg verify gates anchors, coverage, and literal fidelity.

nodes.title: Knowledge graph format
nodes.heading: Shape
nodes.summary: Shape { "version": 2, "generatedAt": "2026-05-30T12:00:00.000Z", "contentHash": "a1b2c3…", "context": "## hev ask\n\nA search overlay for Astro docs…", "glossary": [ { "term": "knowledge graph", "aliases": ["kg"], "defi…

nodes.title: MCP server
nodes.summary: ask mcp turns the committed knowledge graph into stdio MCP tools for coding agents. It can serve a local .hev-ask/kg.json offline or proxy a deployed /api/ask endpoint for fresh graph reads and answer streaming.

nodes.title: MCP server
nodes.heading: Configure
nodes.summary: MCP clients configure ask as the command and pass either --kg-path .hev-ask/kg.json mcp for local reads or --endpoint <url> mcp for a deployed site. The endpoint form is required for the answer tool.

nodes.title: MCP server
nodes.heading: Data sources
nodes.summary: The MCP server follows the CLI data-source rules: --endpoint uses the HTTP read API and POST /api/ask answer path, otherwise --kg-path reads from disk. Local graph reads reload the file on each call so fresh kg.json changes are visible.

nodes.title: MCP server
nodes.heading: Protocol surface
nodes.summary: The MCP implementation speaks newline-delimited JSON-RPC over stdio and handles initialize, tools/list, tools/call, and the initialized notification. Protocol errors return JSON-RPC errors; tool failures return MCP tool results with isError.

nodes.title: MCP server
nodes.heading: Tools
nodes.summary: The MCP server exposes glossary_list, glossary_get, sections_list, section_get, overview, search, and answer. Tool results include readable text plus structuredContent so agents can inspect the original response shape.

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.summary: The SearchOverlay.astro command palette: props, the data-hev-ask-open opener attribute, keyboard model, the mode toggle, and theming through CSS variables. SearchOverlay.astro renders the ⌘K command palette. Add it once…

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.heading: Keyboard model
nodes.summary: Keyboard model The interaction is the core of hev ask's two-path design: Type → debounced keyword search runs; the first result is auto-active. Enter → if you haven't moved the selection, runs the agentic loop ("ask AI"…

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.heading: Keyword results and deep links
nodes.summary: Keyword results and deep links Each keyword result row renders the document title, an optional heading breadcrumb (Concepts › The agentic loop), and a one-line snippet. The row's link is the chunk's url, which already ca…

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.heading: Opening the overlay
nodes.summary: Opening the overlay Two ways, both built in: ⌘K / Ctrl-K is bound automatically once the component is on the page. Any element with data-hev-ask-open opens it on click — wire up as many triggers as you like (header butt…

nodes.summary: Props Prop Type Default Description endpoint string '/api/ask' Endpoint the overlay posts queries to. Must match the integration's endpoint option. placeholder string 'Search the docs…' Placeholder text for the input. d…

nodes.summary: Suggested questions When AI is on, the overlay fetches a short list of suggested questions from the endpoint (GET /api/ask) the first time it opens and shows them in the empty state. They come from the knowledge graph's…

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.heading: The mode toggle
nodes.summary: The mode toggle The overlay persists an "AI on Enter" preference in localStorage under the key hev-ask:mode (agentic or keyword). Readers who never want the model call can flip it off; the choice survives reloads.

nodes.title: SearchOverlay component
nodes.heading: The streamed answer
nodes.summary: The streamed answer Pressing Enter (with a key configured) replaces the keyword rows with an answer panel. The sub-queries the model runs appear live as a faint searched: … line, then the grounded answer streams in token…

nodes.summary: Theming The overlay's markup uses the as- class prefix and reads your page's CSS custom properties. Define these on :root (this site's values shown) and the overlay inherits your palette: :root { --paper: #111111; / over…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.summary: How hev ask works: heading-chunk indexing with real anchors, keyword prefiltering widened by a glossary, the bounded agentic search loop, and the offline knowledge graph. hev ask is three parts: a heading-chunk index w…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: Asking is the default
nodes.summary: Asking is the default The overlay is ask-first. A single word is treated as a keyword lookup and answered instantly from the index. The moment the query grows past one word — the reader types a space — the overlay stops…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: Chunks and anchors
nodes.summary: Chunks and anchors hev ask does not index pages — it indexes sections. Each document is split on its headings (up to a configurable depth, default ## and ###). Content before the first heading becomes the intro chunk wh…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: Degradation, by design
nodes.summary: Degradation, by design hev ask is built to keep working as pieces drop away: No key at runtime → keyword mode only. The overlay still searches. No key at build → the committed kg.json is kept; the build warns but never…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: Keyword search and the glossary
nodes.summary: Keyword search and the glossary As the reader types, the server runs a dependency-free prefilter over the chunks: 1. Expand the query. Each term picks up its glossary aliases (k8s → kubernetes) and the tokens of any matc…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: The agentic search loop
nodes.summary: The agentic search loop When the reader presses Enter (and an API key is present), the query goes to a bounded tool-use loop that ends by streaming a grounded answer. It runs in two phases. Phase 1 — gather. The model is…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: The knowledge graph
nodes.summary: The knowledge graph The knowledge graph (kg.json) is built offline by a strong model (Claude Opus 4.8 by default) and committed to your repo as a reviewable artifact — the agent's view of your docs. It holds: nodes — one…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: The system prompt is cached
nodes.summary: The system prompt is cached The knowledge graph's map and section summaries are injected into the system prompt with a cachecontrol marker, so across the rounds it's a prompt-cache hit rather than re-sent tokens. The ans…

nodes.title: Concepts
nodes.heading: Two ways to build it
nodes.summary: The graph distillation can be produced by the Claude Code skill or by ask kg build with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY. Both paths feed the same deterministic assembler and are hash-gated to avoid model work when content is unchanged.

nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.summary: hev ask turns Astro docs into a committed, section-level knowledge graph that powers both a reader overlay and agent-facing tooling. The graph is built offline, reviewed in git, and used as the shared source for keyword search, grounded answers, and docs-aware integrations.

nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.heading: How it fits an Astro site
nodes.summary: Describes the package pieces: the integration injects search, answer, and read routes; the overlay handles the command palette; the CLI builds, verifies, and reads the graph; and every path degrades when keys or artifacts are missing.

nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.heading: Next steps
nodes.summary: Next steps Quick start — add search to your site in five minutes. Concepts — chunks, anchors, the agentic loop, and the knowledge graph. Tradeoffs and Limits — what you're choosing, and what hev ask deliberately doesn't…

nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.heading: Two surfaces, one graph
nodes.summary: Explains the two consumers of the same committed graph: browser readers use the overlay for keyword results and streamed answers, while coding agents use command, MCP, or client surfaces to inspect terms, sections, and grounded answers without scraping rendered HTML.

nodes.title: Introduction
nodes.heading: Who this is for
nodes.summary: Who this is for You're building or maintaining an Astro 5 docs site. Your content lives in a content collection as Markdown or MDX. You want search that: works out of the box without standing up a service or running a cr…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.summary: The hard boundaries of hev ask: corpus scope, recall ceiling, single-call knowledge-graph build, frontmatter parsing, latency, and adapter requirements — what it deliberately does not do. These are the boundaries you sh…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: A server route is required
nodes.summary: A server route is required The /api/ask endpoint renders on demand, so a fully static build cannot serve search. You need a server or hybrid adapter (Node, Cloudflare, Vercel, etc.). Static-only sites should use a stati…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: Agentic search adds latency
nodes.summary: Agentic search adds latency The agentic path is bounded by maxIterations (default 4) Claude round-trips — worst case a few seconds. It is not instant, by nature. The keyword path is the instant lane and always available;…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: Anchors depend on Astro's slugger
nodes.summary: Deep links depend on github-slugger matching Astro heading ids. ask kg verify is the CI guard that catches missing rendered anchors before broken section links ship.

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: Frontmatter parsing is a flat-YAML subset
nodes.summary: Frontmatter parsing is a flat-YAML subset The offline build parses frontmatter with a small flat-YAML splitter, not a full YAML parser. It handles the common docs schema (string and number fields). Nested frontmatter str…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: Recall has a keyword ceiling
nodes.summary: Recall has a keyword ceiling Retrieval is keyword token-overlap widened by the glossary — not embeddings. The agentic loop grounds its answer in what retrieval surfaces; it cannot ground in — or link to — what retrieval…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: Secrets live server-side
nodes.summary: Secrets live server-side The agentic path needs ANTHROPICAPIKEY in the server environment that runs /api/ask. The key is never exposed to the browser. If the key isn't present at runtime, the endpoint serves keyword res…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: The corpus is your content collection
nodes.summary: The corpus is your content collection hev ask searches the Astro content collection(s) you configure, and nothing else. There is no crawler, no external sitemap ingestion, and no way to index pages that aren't collectio…

nodes.title: Limits
nodes.heading: The knowledge-graph build is a single model call
nodes.summary: The knowledge-graph build is a single model call The offline build sends your full cleaned corpus to the model in one call. For a typical docs site (dozens of pages) this fits comfortably. A very large corpus would excee…

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.summary: Add hev ask to an Astro docs site: install the package, register the integration, drop the SearchOverlay component in your layout, and build the knowledge graph. This walks through adding search to an existing Astro 5 d…

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: 1. Install
nodes.summary: 1. Install Once published, install from npm: pnpm add @hev/ask Until then, consume the latest version straight from the package subdirectory on GitHub: pnpm add "git+ssh://git@github.com/hev/ask.git#main&path:/packages…

nodes.heading: 2. Register the integration

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: 3. Add a server adapter
nodes.summary: 3. Add a server adapter The /api/ask route is rendered on demand. Add whichever adapter matches your host; this site uses Cloudflare: // astro.config.mjs adapter: cloudflare({ platformProxy: { enabled: true } }), // ...…

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: 4. Render the overlay
nodes.summary: 4. Render the overlay Add the component once, somewhere global like your base layout, and give readers a way to open it. Any element with data-hev-ask-open opens the palette, and ⌘K is bound automatically. // src/layout…

facts.literal:
---
// src/layouts/Base.astro
import SearchOverlay from "@hev/ask/components/SearchOverlay.astro";
---
<button type="button" data-hev-ask-open>
  Search <kbd>⌘K</kbd>
</button>

<slot />

<SearchOverlay />

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: 5. Build the knowledge graph
nodes.summary: The quickstart recommends building the graph with the Claude Code skill, which runs ask kg corpus and ask kg assemble without spending the user's API key. The fallback is pnpm exec ask kg build, followed by ask kg verify and committing kg.json.

facts.literal:
You: build the hev ask knowledge graph

Claude runs:
  ask kg corpus                # emits the sections to distil
  …writes context/glossary/summaries/suggestions…
  ask kg assemble              # writes .hev-ask/kg.json (deterministically)

export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...
pnpm exec ask kg build          # writes .hev-ask/kg.json

pnpm exec ask kg verify         # builds the site, checks every anchor resolves
git add .hev-ask/kg.json

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: Prerequisites
nodes.summary: Prerequisites Astro 5 with at least one content collection. A server or hybrid adapter (Node, Cloudflare, Vercel, …). The search endpoint renders on demand, so a fully static build can't serve it. Optional: an ANTHROPICA…

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: Turn on agentic search
nodes.summary: Turn on agentic search Set ANTHROPICAPIKEY in the server environment where /api/ask runs (your host's secrets, or .env in local dev). With a key present, pressing Enter in the overlay runs the agentic loop; without one,…

nodes.title: Quick start
nodes.heading: Verify it works
nodes.summary: Verification checks keyword deep links, the agentic answer stream, and anchor correctness. ask kg verify exits non-zero when generated chunk anchors are missing from the built HTML.

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.summary: What you're choosing by adopting hev ask: the dependency posture, the committed knowledge graph, the cost and latency of agentic search, and how it compares to Pagefind, Algolia, and Orama. Every search tool makes choic…

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.heading: A committed knowledge graph
nodes.summary: A committed knowledge graph The knowledge graph is generated offline and committed to git as JSON, rather than computed at runtime or hidden in a service. Upside: it's reviewable in pull requests, deterministic at runtim…

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.heading: Cost and latency of agentic search
nodes.summary: Cost and latency of agentic search The agentic path calls Claude. That means real, if small, money and latency: Latency: worst case is roughly maxIterations Haiku round-trips (a few seconds). The keyword path stays insta…

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.heading: How it compares
nodes.summary: How it compares Tool Retrieval AI ranking Deep links Hosting hev ask keyword + glossary yes, on Enter heading anchors your adapter + optional API key Pagefind keyword (static index) no page / anchor static, none Algolia…

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.heading: Keyword retrieval, not embeddings
nodes.summary: Keyword retrieval, not embeddings Retrieval is dependency-free token overlap widened by the glossary — no embeddings, no vector store. Upside: nothing to host, nothing to keep in sync, edge-safe, and instant. The glossar…

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.heading: One dependency, deliberately
nodes.summary: One dependency, deliberately hev ask aims to be close to zero-dependency, with one exception: github-slugger (3 KB, pure JS, edge-safe). Generating heading anchors by hand risks drifting from Astro's renderer and shippi…

nodes.title: Tradeoffs
nodes.heading: Two paths instead of one
nodes.summary: Two paths instead of one hev ask runs an instant keyword path and an agentic path, and asks the reader to choose between them by pressing Enter. The upside is that the common case (a word or two) stays instant and keyle…
